claims.report_anchor: ## 1. Paper Identification and Source Package Used
claims.statement: The paper was submitted to NeurIPS 2026 (uses neurips_2026.sty).

evidences.notes: Document class confirms NeurIPS 2026 submission format.

claims.report_anchor: ## 1. Paper Identification and Source Package Used
claims.statement: Full LaTeX source was available: 737-line main.tex, macro.tex, style, bibliography, figures.

evidences.quote_text: Files: main.tex (51325 bytes, 737 lines), macro.tex, neurips_2026.sty, reference.bib, 8 PDF figures
evidences.notes: All source files extracted and verified.

claims.statement: Hybrid SSM-attention LLMs fail on deep reasoning over evicted context due to insufficient computation, not memory capacity.

evidences.quote_text: We find that the performance of vanilla SSM-attention hybrid models degrades (under the same token budget) as the required reasoning depth increases even when the amount of information to store is held fixed.
evidences.notes: Abstract-level statement of the core finding.

evidences.quote_text: Contrary to these works, we show that such models can fail as the required reasoning depth to solve a task increases, even when the amount of information to store is held fixed.
evidences.notes: Related work section clarifies the distinction from prior capacity-focused work.

evidences.quote_text: Our key insight is that recurrence can be used not only for prediction but also for memory consolidation.

claims.statement: The core conceptual replacement: treat fast-weight formation as a two-phase process with multi-pass offline refinement.

evidences.quote_text: We introduce a sleep during LLM training, in which the model performs recursion during a consolidation phase, before evicting tokens from attention layers once the context window is full.
Algorithm 1: LLM sleep training with hard eviction.

claims.statement: The paper contains no formal theoretical results (no theorems, proofs, or complexity bounds).

evidences.notes: Theorem environments absent from the entire LaTeX source.

claims.statement: The paper would face questions about practical significance: N=6 loops vs no-loop on GSM 8-op only gains ~10.5% relative at 6x training cost.

evidences.quote_text: For Jet, six loops improves final accuracy on six-operation problems from 0.742 to 0.812 and on eight-operation problems from 0.351 to 0.388.
evidences.notes: Raw accuracy numbers from GSM-Infinite experiments.

evidences.quote_text: Training cost grows roughly linearly with the number of recurrent steps N.
